title: How to calculate simple interest
task: Interest that doesn't compound — flat-rate loans, short-term notes, bonds' accrued interest.

excel.note: A2=principal, B2=annual rate, C2=years. Total repayment: =A2*(1+B2*C2).

explanation: Simple interest is principal × rate × time: $5,000 at 4% for 3 years earns $600, because the interest never joins the principal. Compare with compounding (see the compound-interest recipe): the same money compounded monthly grows by $637 — the gap widens with time and rate. Partial years just go in as fractions: 90 days is C2 = 90/365 (or /360 under some loan conventions — check the contract).
